description: The strategy uses to open the issue
A pattern to match on the connection name or array of connection IDs. Use `*` as wildcard.
The minimum log level to open the issue on
A pattern to match on the transformation name or array of transformation IDs. Use `*` as wildcard.
The minimum delay (backpressure) to open the issue for min of 1 minute (60000) and max of 1 day (86400000)
A pattern to match on the destination name or array of destination IDs. Use `*` as wildcard.
An array of rejection causes to match on, or '*' to match all rejection causes.
Optional array of rejection causes to exclude. When specified, rejection_causes must be '*'. This allows matching all causes EXCEPT the specified ones.
A pattern to match on the source name or array of source IDs. Use `*` as wildcard.

A unique, human-friendly name for the transformation
Key-value environment variables to be passed to the transformation
Date the transformation was last updated
Algorithm to use when calculating delay between retries
A retry rule must be of type `retry`
Time in MS between each retry
Maximum number of retries to attempt
HTTP codes to retry on. Accepts: range expressions (e.g., "400-499", ">400"), specific codes (e.g., 404), and exclusions (e.g., "!401"). Example: ["500-599", ">400", 404, "!401"]
JSON using our filter syntax to filter on request headers
A filter rule must be of type `filter`
A transformation rule must be of type `transform`
ID of the attached transformation object. Optional input, always set once the rule is defined
The unique name of the transformation
A string representation of your JavaScript (ES6) code to run
A key-value object of environment variables to encrypt and expose to your transformation code
You can optionally define a new transformation while creating a transform rule
A delay rule must be of type `delay`
A deduplicate rule must be of type `deduplicate`
Time window in milliseconds for deduplicate

Fields to include when generating deduplicate key. Supports root fields (e.g., "headers"), dot notation (e.g., "body.user.id"), array wildcards (e.g., "body.items[*].sku"), and array indices (e.g., "body.items[0].name"). Array notation must be followed by a property name.

Fields to exclude when generating deduplicate key. Supports root fields (e.g., "headers"), dot notation (e.g., "body.user.id"), array wildcards (e.g., "body.items[*].sku"), and array indices (e.g., "body.items[0].name"). Array notation must be followed by a property name.
Unique name of the connection for this source
Full name of the connection concatenated from source, connection and desitnation name
Array of rules configured on the connection
Date the connection was last updated
The custom hostname to attach to the project

name: Issue Triggers
description: Issue Triggers lets you setup rules that trigger issues when certain conditions are met.

name: Attempts
description: An attempt is any request that Hookdeck makes on behalf of an event.

name: Bookmarks
description: A bookmark lets you conveniently store and replay a specific request.

name: Destinations
description: A destination is any endpoint to which your webhooks can be routed.

name: Bulk cancel events
description: Bulk cancel operations allow you to cancel multiple pending events at once.

name: Bulk retry events
description: Bulk retry operations allow you to retry many events at once. A bulk retry is first estimated, then created, then processed asynchronously.

name: Events
description: An event is any request that Hookdeck receives from a source.

name: Bulk retry ignored events
description: Bulk retry ignored events allow you to retry many events that were previously ignored (for instance due to filter rules) at once.

name: Integrations
description: An integration configures platform-specific behaviors, such as signature verification.

name: Issues
description: Issues lets you track problems in your project and communicate resolution steps with your team.

name: Metrics
description: Query aggregated metrics for events, requests, and attempts with time-based grouping and filtering.

name: Requests
description: A request represent a webhook received by Hookdeck.

name: Bulk retry requests
description: Bulk retry requests allow you to re-ingest many previously received requests at once.

name: Sources
description: A source represents any third party that sends webhooks to Hookdeck.

name: Transformations
description: A transformation represents JavaScript code that will be executed on a connection's requests. Transformations are applied to connections using Rules.

name: Connections
description: A connection lets you route webhooks from a source to a destination, using a rule.

name: Notifications
description: Notifications let your team receive alerts anytime an issue changes.

This endpoint pauses a connection. A paused connection will still receive events, but those events will be marked with a status of `HOLD` and will be delivered only once the connection is unpaused.

This endpoint unpauses a connection. Any event with a status of `HOLD` will be attempted. Destination delivery rate will be respected. This operation is asynchronous, so it may take some time for all events to be `QUEUED` for delivery.
